journey.summary: Travel from Kolkata to Mumbai by train/bus. Duration: 4-6 hours. Cost: $40

places.name: Gateway of India
places.description: An iconic arch monument built in the early 20th century to commemorate the landing of King George V and Queen Mary at Apollo Bunder during their visit to India in 1911. It stands as a symbol of Mumbai.

places.name: Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj Terminus (CSMT)
places.description: A UNESCO World Heritage Site and a historic railway station in Mumbai, known for its Victorian Gothic Revival architecture blended with traditional Indian elements. It's one of the busiest railway stations in India.

places.name: Elephanta Caves
places.description: A collection of cave temples predominantly dedicated to the Hindu god Shiva, located on Elephanta Island. These rock-cut sculptures date back to the 5th to 8th centuries and are a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

places.name: Haji Ali Dargah
places.description: A mosque and dargah (tomb) located on an islet off the coast of Worli. It houses the tomb of Pir Haji Ali Shah Bukhari and is a revered pilgrimage site, accessible via a narrow causeway during low tide.
places.address: Dargah Road, Lala Lajpatrai Marg, Worli, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400026

places.name: Bandra Fort (Castella de Aguada)
places.description: A historic Portuguese watchtower and fort built in 1640. It offers panoramic views of the Arabian Sea, Bandra-Worli Sea Link, and Mahim Bay, making it a popular spot for sunset views.
places.address: Byramji Jeejeebhoy Road, Bandstand, Bandra West, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400050

places.name: Dr. Bhau Daji Lad Museum
places.description: Mumbai's oldest museum, housed in a magnificent Palladian-style building. It showcases the city's cultural heritage and history through a collection of archaeological finds, maps, historical photographs, and industrial arts.
places.address: 91 A, Rani Baug, Veer Mata Jijabai Bhonsle Udyan, Dr Baba Saheb Ambedkar Rd, Byculla East, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400027
